skill.name: meta-analysis-methods-generator
skill.description: Generates the Methods section for a meta-analysis paper, including search strategy, screening, quality assessment, data extraction, and statistical analysis.
skill.summary: Generates structured Methods sections for meta-analysis papers with search strategy, screening, quality assessment, data extraction, and statistical analysis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 53 static analysis findings are false positives. The high-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections matched harmless descriptive text in YAML frontmatter and medical research methodology documentation (tool names like ROB2, NOS, PROBAST are risk-of-bias assessment instruments, not cryptographic hashes). The medium-severity 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are all Markdown formatting characters — inline code backticks around file names and template variables, plus triple-backtick code blocks for bash documentation examples. The 'hardcoded URL' is a documentation citation link to the source repository. The 'system reconnaissance' detections match text in a pre-existing audit report JSON file. The skill is a purely template-driven prompt workflow for generating academic text; the single packaged script (validate_skill.py) is a trivial argparse wrapper that prints a validation message with no file system, network, or execution capabilities.

content.value_statement: Writing a meta-analysis methods section is time-consuming and requires precise methodological structure. This skill generates complete, well-organized Methods sections with search strategy, screening, quality assessment, data extraction, and statistical analysis in English or Chinese.
actual_capabilities: Generates complete Methods sections for meta-analysis papers with six required subsections in the correct order
Produces search strategy content describing PubMed API integration and keyword-based search construction
Creates quality assessment sections supporting ROB2, NOS, ROBINS-I, ROBINS-E, QUIPS, and PROBAST risk of bias tools
Generates statistical analysis content covering heterogeneity assessment with I-squared thresholds and publication bias detection via funnel plots
Outputs all generated content in either English or Chinese based on the user language parameter
Follows a structured workflow from input validation through subsection generation to final document compilation
limitations: Generated output is template-driven and requires researcher review for domain-specific accuracy and alignment with the actual study protocol
Does not perform real literature searches, run R statistical packages, or compute any quantitative analyses
Cannot validate the scientific accuracy or completeness of user-provided inclusion criteria or PICOS parameters
Quality assessment tool selection is inferred from study type keywords and may need manual correction for complex or mixed study designs

use_cases.title: Academic Researcher Writing a Manuscript
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing a meta-analysis for journal submission generates a properly structured Methods section that follows methodological reporting standards and includes all six required subsections.

use_cases.title: Systematic Review Team Standardizing Output
use_cases.description: A research team conducting multiple systematic reviews uses this skill to ensure consistent Methods section formatting, structure, and completeness across all their projects.

use_cases.title: Peer Reviewer Checking Methodology Completeness
use_cases.description: A journal reviewer or editor generates a reference Methods section outline to quickly verify that a submitted manuscript includes all essential methodological components.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Complete Methods Section
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a methods section for a meta-analysis on [topic]. Use these PICOS criteria: Population: [population], Intervention: [intervention], Comparison: [comparison], Outcome: [outcome], Study design: [study design]. Use the following inclusion criteria: [criteria]. Use [tool name] for quality assessment. Output in English.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start for first-time users who need a full Methods section

prompt_templates.title: Search Strategy Subsection Only
prompt_templates.prompt: Write only the search strategy subsection for my meta-analysis on [topic]. My PICOS keywords are: [keywords]. Use the PubMed API as the primary search database and describe how the keywords were combined into a search string. Output in English.
prompt_templates.scenario: When only the search strategy subsection needs to be generated

prompt_templates.title: Quality Assessment with Tool Recommendation
prompt_templates.prompt: My meta-analysis includes the following study types: [RCT, observational, or etiological studies]. Based on my PICOS: Population=[], Intervention=[], Comparison=[], Outcome=[], Study design=[], recommend the most appropriate risk of bias assessment tool. Then write the quality assessment subsection describing each domain of the selected tool. Output in English.
prompt_templates.scenario: When unsure which quality assessment tool to use for the study design

prompt_templates.title: Complete Methods Section in Chinese
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete meta-analysis methods section in Chinese. Topic: [topic in Chinese]. PICOS: Population=[], Intervention=[], Comparison=[], Outcome=[], Study design=[]. Inclusion criteria: [criteria]. Exclusion criteria: [exclusion criteria]. Quality assessment tool: [tool name]. Use formal academic Chinese throughout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: generating the complete section in Chinese for Chinese-language journals

output_examples.input: Generate a methods section for a meta-analysis on cognitive behavioral therapy for anxiety disorders in adults
output_examples.output: The output is a complete Methods section with six clearly labeled subsections: Search Strategy, Inclusion and Exclusion Criteria, Literature Screening, Quality Assessment, Data Extraction, and Statistical Analysis. Each subsection follows academic writing conventions with appropriate methodological detail and consistent terminology throughout.

output_examples.input: Write the quality assessment section using ROB2 for a meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials on exercise interventions for depression
output_examples.output: The quality assessment subsection describes each ROB2 domain in detail: random sequence generation, allocation concealment, blinding of participants and personnel, blinding of outcome assessment, incomplete outcome data, and selective reporting. Each domain includes a description of how bias risk was evaluated.

output_examples.input: Generate a complete methods section in Chinese for a meta-analysis on acupuncture treatment for chronic pain
output_examples.output: The complete Methods section is generated in formal academic Chinese with all six subsections. The content follows the same structured outline as the English version while using appropriate Chinese academic terminology for each methodological concept and maintaining consistency in tone and style throughout the document.

best_practices: Provide complete and specific PICOS criteria with clear population descriptions, intervention details, and outcome measures for the most accurate and relevant generated content

Review all generated content against your actual study protocol and adjust methodological details such as database names, screening procedures, and statistical thresholds to match what was actually performed
Specify the correct risk of bias assessment tool explicitly based on your study designs rather than relying solely on automatic inference from study type keywords
anti_patterns: Do not submit the generated Methods section without thorough researcher review and verification that all methodological details match the actual study procedures
Do not use the skill to fabricate search result counts, study inclusion numbers, or any quantitative data that should come from actual research conduct
Do not skip providing clear inclusion and exclusion criteria as these are essential inputs that shape the accuracy of the generated methods content

faq.question: What inputs do I need to provide to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need to provide PICOS criteria (Population, Intervention, Comparison, Outcome, Study design), inclusion and exclusion criteria, the quality assessment tool name or study type, and your preferred output language (English or Chinese).

faq.question: Which risk of bias assessment tools does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports ROB2 for randomized controlled trials, ROBINS-I for non-randomized intervention studies, NOS for observational studies, ROBINS-E for etiological studies, and QUIPS or PROBAST for prognostic studies. It can also help recommend the appropriate tool based on your study design.

faq.question: Does this skill actually search PubMed or run statistical analyses in R?
faq.answer: No. This skill generates the text of the Methods section describing how searches and analyses would be conducted. It does not perform real literature searches, execute R statistical packages, or compute any quantitative results. You must conduct the actual research and analyses independently.

faq.question: Can the output be generated in languages other than English and Chinese?
faq.answer: Currently the skill supports English and Chinese output only. The template structure is consistent across both languages. Support for additional languages may be added in future versions.

faq.question: How should I verify the accuracy of the generated Methods section?
faq.answer: Review each subsection against your actual study protocol. Confirm that the search strategy reflects your real search process, the screening procedure matches your team workflow, the quality assessment tool aligns with your study designs, and all statistical analysis parameters match what you used.

faq.question: What should I do if the skill recommends the wrong quality assessment tool for my study?
faq.answer: If the automatically inferred tool is incorrect, specify the correct tool name directly in your input to override the automatic selection. For studies with mixed designs, you may need to generate separate quality assessment subsections for each study type.
